translation.en: This bottle is full of water.
This bottle is full of water.

notes_md:
**đầy** means **full** — no more space inside something. It describes containers, places, or even feelings.

It usually comes **after the noun**: *cốc đầy*, *bình đầy*.  
You also use **đầy + noun** to mean “full of …” (a lot inside).


Strong opposites: **vơi / cạn** (less and less, for liquids), **trống** (empty space).  
For extra emphasis, you’ll hear **đầy ắp** = very full, almost overflowing.
exampleSentencesTranslated: My glass is full so I won't pour any more.
The restaurant is very crowded today, every table is full of people.
My parents often tell me to keep my wallet full of money when going out.
exampleSentences: Ly nước của em đã đầy nên em không rót thêm nữa.
Nhà hàng hôm nay rất đông, bàn nào cũng đầy người.
Ba mẹ thường bảo anh phải giữ ví luôn đầy tiền khi ra ngoài.

example.es: Esta botella está llena de agua.
example.en: This bottle is full of water.

definitions.en: Having no empty space; filled to capacity.
definitions.es: Sin espacio vacío; lleno hasta el límite.
definitions.vi: Không có khoảng trống; đầy đủ.
